L1.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the experimental plugin mode of the VPN authentication proxy. When a client that does not support web-based single sign-on attempts to connect, the authentication logic correctly issues a deny decision and writes a denial flag to the control file. However, the plugin returns a synchronous success status code to the VPN server instead of an error. Because the VPN server only consults the control file when the plugin defers, the synchronous success return is interpreted as immediate approval, granting the unauthenticated client full network access.

ground_truth.reason: The plugin's client-deny handler returns a success status code instead of an error, causing the VPN server to treat denied clients as authenticated and granting them full network access without completing OIDC authentication.
